entry.title: Re: Custom Price Value Not Updated in Credit Memo Request w.r.t Billing

entry.title: Re: Incorrect Display of License Type in Business User an...

Dear all,

this can not be checked via a community post. Please open a case with the query and mention the component SRD-CC-CI-CCS.

The technical support needs to check this.

entry.title: Re: Ad-hoc Service with T&M billing (Time & Material)
entry.content: Is there a checklist functionality in Service Request, like in Old CRM Service requests

entry.title: Re: Fiscal Year Change in co.code 8810 possible only after year-end closing 2023
entry.content: Hello, thank you for your question. It has been forwarded to the experts. Kind regards, Henrike

entry.title: Re: SAP XI/PI excel to custom RFC
entry.content:
Hi,

it's a problem that i know very well. The signature of the XML-RFC is somewhere cached and i haven't yet found a way to invalid the cache. You have to rename the communication channel that is used - than it's works again. Just a new activation of the CC isn't enough to reload the signature. 

I guess you are consuming an RFC lookup from a mapping, correct? I my case it was a SOAP lookup and giving the channel a new name solved the issue.

BR HElmut

entry.title: Re: 401 Unauthorized - Build Apps Unable to Access BTP De...
entry.content: Thanks Dan For your inputs. I changed Authentication Type to "OAuth2JWTBearer" also I found I missed one step of adding the Destination in the Destination section of SAP Build control Tower. Its working as expected now. But one important thing is in SAP Help Documentation" https://help.sap.com/docs/build-apps/service-guide/sap-build-process-automation?q=service+key%22" its clearly mentioned to set authentication type must be set as "OAuth2ClientCredentials" so I followed SAP provided steps

entry.title: Re: 401 Unauthorized - Build Apps Unable to Access BTP De...
entry.content: Thanks Dinu for your Inputs. I changed Authentication Type to "OAuth2JWTBearer" also I found I missed one step of adding the Destination in the Destination section of SAP Build control Tower. Its working as expected now

entry.title: Re: Universal Allocation on the basis of revenue
entry.content: Hello, thank you for your question. It has been forwarded to the experts. Kind regards, Henrike

entry.title: EWAの削除/無効化手順
entry.content:
特定インスタンスのEWA削除手順について、ご連絡をお願いします。

よろしくお願いいたします。

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: EWAの削除/無効化手順
entry.content:
ご担当者　様

平素よりお世話になっております。

ある特定システムのEWA機能を無効化するように、下記操作をご実施頂ければ。

①T-cd：solman＿setup->Application Operations->Earlywatch Alert Management->管理対象システムSIDを選定する->step 2 Activate EWAオプションにチェックを外す➞保存する。
➁管理対象システムのSDCCN➞「To do 」タブに、タスク「Maintenance Package」 と 「SAP Earlywatch Alert」があれば、削除してください。
➂管理対象システムのSDCCN➞メニュー -> Goto -> Settings -> Task-specific->RFC　destinationにて、Solman指定のBACK RFCへの「Active」チェックボクスにチェックを外す➞保存する。

以上、ご確認のほどよろしくお願いいたします。
SAPサポート
ショウセイ

entry.title: Re: EWM - Auto create Warehouse Task from Inbound & Outbound
entry.content:
Hi @Dang_Bao_Van,

Can I create auto WT for only outbound delivery which volume over for example 10 m3 using PPF?

entry.title: Re: In S4, the main table for financial data is ACDOCA, S...
entry.content:
Hello MK,

you might want to have a look at this blog post, where the different roles of ACDOCA and BSEG in S/4HANA are explained. TL;DR: ACDOCA is the universal journal entry in S/4HANA (replacing this role that BSEG had in ECC), BSEG is by and large just an open item registry (+ a data entry view for manually posted FI documents) in S/4HANA. Thus you should summarize the BSEG items as much as possible in S/4HANA.

Kind regards, 

Stefan

entry.title: Re: LE-TRA versus Transportation Management
entry.content:
Hello @Peter2408 

Logistics TM integration is defined in SPRO: Transportation Management -> Integration -> Logistics Integration -> ...

The integration is defined per:

  • sales organization, distribution channel, division, sales doc. type, shipping condition - for sales documents (sales orders mainly)
  • purchasing organization, purchasing group, order type - for purchasing documents
  • shipping point, delivery type, shipping condition - for deliveries.

That is more or less the equivalent of transportation relevance from LE-TRA.

 

Freight unit defines goods transported together. You can view FUs as transpor requirements, goods that need to be shipped.

Freight units are not really obligatory, although they are used in most scenarios. You can have TM create freight orders directly from logistics documents, i.e. sales orders, purchase orders and/or deliveries.

Transportation unit is another object in TM, distinct from freight unit. It is used in load consolidation and it models e.g. containers or trailers. A typical scenario is that freight units are consolidated into transportation units.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Hiring 2 employees on the same position, the first one is successful and the last one is failing
entry.content:
Hi, 

For my new client they are facing issues when hiring two employees on the same position. Both employees are successfully transferred from recruitment to onboarding. The first employee is successfully hired in EC, but the second employee is failing. 

Any thought on what the issue could be?

entry.title: Calling same story different users different colors
entry.content: Two different users call up the same SAP Analytics Cloud Story, the diagram (chart) colors are different for both. Why is that so? The original color is green. For the other user it turns to orange and blue. What could be the reason?

entry.title: Re: AFC is always running in test mode for depreciation
entry.content:
Hi, you need to maintain at the backend config the test flag with the parameter type TESTR. Then it will work.

As you start it right now, it is standard behavior of the depreciation program.

See also the AFC Content which is shipped by SAP. For programs with a few fields on selection screen it works very well. For other programs like FAGL_FCV where you have got many fields, tabs to be filled, it is more likely to use custom AFC Content.

Regards

Thomas

entry.title: Re: During our recent physical verification, we identified some excess assets that were not part of
entry.content: try to use tc ABNAN

entry.title: MS-Word as editor getting error
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I would like to use Word as an editor in SAP.
We have already installed Note 1091389 - MS Word in SAPscript: Installing new WordTemplate.dot.

The document from SAP is also signed according to our basis.

I get the following message when I go to the editor in SO10, for example:

Error loading the document
Message no. SOFFICEINTEGRATION142

Diagnosis
An internal error has occurred in SAP Document Container Control when loading the document.

Procedure
If this error occurs repeatedly, please enter an error message. Follow the corresponding instructions for creating the log file using the SAPROFFICEINTEGRATIONTRACE1 report.

 


The Standard SAP Editor is then opened automatically. What can I do?

 

best regards,

entry.title: Re: Working with HANA CV / MDS Cubes. Do not see any addi...
entry.content: Hi, seeing only the dimension name when creating the live data model sounds correct to me. When you now create e.g. a story based on the live model - do you still not see the individual attributes? When selecting a dimension there you should be able to expand the dimension and see the attributes.

entry.title: Re: Maintain Inspection types : Config Node Not Editable...
entry.content: Hi, it is not permitted to make changes to SAP configurations. This means that the inspection types delivered by SAP cannot be changed. You would have to create your own customer-specific inspection types. Alternatively, BADI BADI_INSPLOT_PLAN_ASSIGNMENT could be used. Extract from the documentation: This Business Add-In (BAdI) is used in the Quality Inspection (QM-IM) component. You can use this BAdI to change the inspection plan that was determined by the system to execute the inspection. Hope this helps. BR Ingo

entry.title: Re: How to update a RAP object within another RAP-object?
entry.content: Yes, I'm using a managed scenario with draft and there we don't have the use case to modify an other BO by clicking an "Save"

entry.title: Re: How to update a RAP object within another RAP-object?
entry.content: please see next comment 😄

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere Sample Content for FI, HR and SD
entry.content:
@silke_jahr @dspokoinyi 
We also had recent issues this week when we initially wanted to install the data product in our SAP_CONTENT Space. It didn't work out and we get an error.


One other run where we wanted to delete the product then from the space ended up in a loop and is running since 4 days (or at least has this status) and can't be stopped...

entry.title: Re: How to update a RAP object within another RAP-object?
entry.content:
The "additonal_save" is as far as I know intendend for your use case to update other database tables, raising an RAP Event, ... . Based on this documentation https://help.sap.com/docs/abap-cloud/abap-rap/implementing-additional-save it seems that currently only the direct update / insert of the database table is working ( without any commit statement ). An EML Statement seems here not to be allowed. This is of course "tricky" when you are working with a draft- enabled BO

Here is maybe a helpful definition of the use cases https://help.sap.com/docs/abap-cloud/abap-rap/additional-save

entry.title: Re: Condition using a String function
entry.content: @MartinaKolafova Thank you for your assistance, you are a superstar. The For-each loop worked perfectly. The true/false that precede the document names are the evaluations of the endsWith statement.

it show up like 1000X_XX_XX instead of H109/1000/X_XX_XX

Kindly help.

entry.title: Re: Join the "SAP Joule Activation for SAP SuccessFactors" Adoption Lab
entry.content: Is it only for me or is hardly any link working in the Joule Setup Guide mentioned in this blog? I especially wanted to check the prerequisites but the link says its not valid (https://help.sap.com/docs/joule/serviceguide/prerequisites)

entry.title: Individual alert in SAP B1
entry.content:
Hi,

I would like to assign an alert to several users, but the alert should only be received by the user associated with the document.

I use user query:

SELECT 'In' + CAST(DATEDIFF(DAY, CAST(GETDATE() AS DATE),CAST(T1.[CLOSE] AS DATE)) AS nvarchar(MAX)) +' days the task '+ UPPER(CAST(T1.DSCRIPTION AS nvarchar(MAX))) + ' deadline is approaching in project' + UPPER(CAST(T0.Name AS NVARCHAR(MAX)))
FROM
OPMG T0 join PMG1 T1 on T1.AbsEntry = T0.AbsEntry
WHERE CAST(T1.[CLOSE] AS DATE) <= CAST(DATEADD(DAY, 6, GETDATE()) AS DATE) AND CAST(T1.[CLOSE] AS DATE) > CAST(GETDATE() AS DATE)
AND T1.FINISH <> 'Y'
AND T1.U_alert = 'Y'
AND (select UserId FROM OHEM WHERE empID = T1.[OWNER]) = $[USER]

The problem is the syntax $[USER] is not working in alerts.

Do you know how to solve this problem?

entry.title: 'Scenario' input field is greyed out; so, "SAP Central Business Configuration" can't be selected
entry.content: I'm trying to go through 'Integrating SAP Central Business Configuration' (https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-alm/setup-administration/central-business-configuration?locale=en-US), and in the Step 4 of 'Integration During Project Creation', since the 'Scenario' input field is greyed out "SAP Central Business Configuration" can't be selected; please advise.

entry.title: Re: How the proposed PIR is calculated in Maintain PIR
entry.content:
System take first month that have sales quantity (M9.2024) till current month (M1.2025). It's 5 months. I tested with below data and it takes 8 months:

M6.2024: 50

M7.2024: 0

M8.2024: 0

M9.2024: 0

M10.2024: 0

M11.2024: 200

M12.2024: 300

M1.2025: 0

--> Proposed PIR for M2.2025 = 69

entry.title: Re: Package.json Script option to force deploy HDI artifacts(Deployement option in SAP HDI)
entry.content:
I have faced a similar issue in the past that I had to redeploy all .hdbsynomym and .hdbsynonymconfig following a schema mapping change in a user provided service.
The method I used was a SQLScript that was getting first all objects that were supposed to be redeployed using the LIST_DEPLOYED HDI API and then use MAKE API to redeploy them.
It was working fine.

entry.title: Re: how to create a file excel .xlsx
entry.content: Had the OP not excluded libraries, that would have been my answer as well. The only limited option available is to create an ALV and use the standard interface to dump this to XLSX. Inflexible, not particularly nice but it works within the constrains of the question.

entry.title: Re: The 15 Most Important Topics for the ABAP Developer of the Future!
entry.content:
Hello @chaouki_akir 

I understand your point of view, but in this blog, the idea is to look to the future. Although IDOC is stable and widely used at the moment, it is very old and is no longer an option for ABAP Environment on BTP and S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, for example. Yes, certainly some integrations are broader with IDOC, as I mentioned earlier, it is quite old. But SAP is improving integrations using other technologies

entry.title: Re: Filtering of cluster table through include program from SLT
entry.content:
Below include work for initial load, but not for delta load. 

Have you tested the delta? 

 

 In this program there is include and exclude to filter the data

***&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
***& Include ZSLT_UPDATE_WITH_FILTER
***&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
***" Event BEGIN OF RECORD code:

IF ( <wa_s_konv>-kappl EQ 'DB' AND
( <wa_s_konv>-kschl <> 'ZK01' AND <wa_s_konv>-kschl <> 'ZORA' AND  <wa_s_konv>-kschl <> 'ZPC1' AND  <wa_s_konv>-kschl <> 'ZPCA' AND
<wa_s_konv>-kschl <> 'ZPDH' AND <wa_s_konv>-kschl <> 'ZPDT' AND <wa_s_konv>-kschl <> 'ZPHD' AND <wa_s_konv>-kschl <> 'ZPOS' AND
<wa_s_konv>-kschl <> 'ZPL1' AND <wa_s_konv>-kschl <> 'ZPL2' AND <wa_s_konv>-kschl <> 'ZPL3' AND <wa_s_konv>-kschl <> 'ZPMA' AND
<wa_s_konv>-kschl <> 'ZPMH' AND <wa_s_konv>-kschl <> 'ZPMP' AND  <wa_s_konv>-kschl <> 'ZPUM') ).

 

<wa_r_konv> = <wa_s_konv>.

ELSE.

SKIP_RECORD.

ENDIF.

entry.title: Re: 401 Unauthorized - Build Apps Unable to Access BTP De...
entry.content:
ClientCredentials will work, but the metadata showing who started the process will be the Build Process Automation service itself; with JWTBearToken or UserTokenExchange you then know who triggered he process.

I do not see anything about adding the destination to the Control Tower in that documentation topic. Where did you see it?

entry.title: Re: Difference in the value of the condition associated with the withholding tax between SD/FI docum
entry.content: Would you be able to share the screenshots proving an illustration of the Amounts?

entry.title: Re: Time Valuation Results - Project Controlling Object
entry.content:
Hi Nicolai, 

Mid last year we delivered the solution for this. Here is more info: https://community.sap.com/t5/product-and-customer-updates/s-4hana-my-timesheet-v4-powered-by-sap-successfactors-time-management/ba-p/13652278 

We also have a roadmap item, in case the customer needs to route all assigned cost to cost objects through payroll (post timesheet approval). This is only required in a few industries. So please check first if your customer can work with the link I posted, and if not, then point them to the H2 2025 roadmap item. Hope this helps? Cheers, Frans

entry.content: Hello Eduard, the attribute is already define navigational

entry.title: ADT (Eclipse) ABAP breadcrumb gets lost sometimes
entry.content:
Today, my Eclipse ADT (2024-06) was showing a wrong breadcrumb for ABAP code.

Instead of displaying while > case > when > if as below

it started to display nothing, or displayed "case > when > if" but not "while", etc.

I installed Eclipse/ADT 2024-12, and I don't see the problem anymore.

How to solve with 2024-06?

Eclipse: 2024-06 (corresponding ADT version)

Backend version: 7.58

entry.title: Account is currently blocked by user RFC_ALE during IDOC creation

Hi ALE/IDOC guys,

Please kindly provide some comments or suggestions on my locking error below:

Account is currently blocked by user RFC_ALE.

This kind of error does not occur all the time, but it was encountered by my teammate once.

What is the root cause of this and if you experienced this before, how were you able to avoid this error?

Let me know guys.

Newcloudguy

entry.title: Urgent: Incorrect Date Handling for Persian Leap Year 1403 in SAP System
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support Team,

We are writing to report a critical issue regarding the handling of the Persian (Jalali) calendar year 1403 in our SAP system. We have identified that the system incorrectly represents the end of the year.

Specifically, while the year 1403 is a leap year in the Persian calendar (meaning it has 366 days), our SAP system is currently displaying the last day of the year as “30 Esfand” (March 20th or 21st depending on the exact year) instead of the correct “30 Esfand” followed by “1 Farvardin” (March 21st or 22nd depending on the exact year). This is causing a significant problem because the leap day (an extra day in the last month) is not being recognized.

The incorrect date handling will have a direct impact on:

Financial Reporting: Inaccurate date calculations will lead to incorrect financial period reporting.
Scheduling: Scheduled tasks and events based on the Persian calendar will be out of sync.
Data Integrity: Data associated with dates in the year 1403 will be inaccurate.
To resolve this issue, we require your immediate assistance in the following:

Identifying the Root Cause: We need to understand why the SAP system is not correctly recognizing the leap year for the Persian calendar year 1403.
Providing a Solution: We need guidance on how to adjust the system to accurately reflect the 366 days in the year 1403, including the leap day.
Implementation Guidance: Please provide clear instructions on how to implement the solution, including any necessary patches, updates, or configuration changes.
We believe this is a critical issue that needs to be resolved urgently to ensure the accuracy of our data and business operations. We would appreciate your prompt response and assistance in resolving this matter.

Thank you for your attention to this important issue.

Sincerely,

entry.title: Re: ADT (Eclipse) ABAP breadcrumb gets lost sometimes
entry.content:
I solved by closing the ABAP source code and reopening it (no need to restart Eclipse). I guess that when opening the source code, ADT starts the ABAP parsing from scratch to build the breadcrumb, while there's a buggy optimization if you change the same ABAP source code again and again without closing.

NB:

  • The actual bug was in the "Local types" tab of a class pool, so maybe switching between tabs of the same class pool could also solve the issue?
  • I saw the ADT bug in Eclipse 2024-06, but I feel it still exists in more recent versions. I have just installed 2024-12, so I will update the answer in a few weeks (or if I see the bug again).

entry.title: Re: Reorg during Compensation Cycle
entry.content: Thank you a lot! This worked... I wanted to be safe with just updating specific worksheets... But with updating all worksheets this worked 🙂 Thank you!

entry.title: Re: Remote table not reflecting measures properly in SAP...
entry.content:
Hey Martin!

Thank you very much for your tips! It makes sense, although I was hoping for an actual fix like a KBA or something from SAP. We opened a ticket for this meanwhile as well.
What I noticed further is that the measures are affected when the data is pushed to the ODQ data units.
I am wondering if the issue relies somehow on the SAP HANA SDI subscriber.

Thank you again for taking from your time to give an advice!

 

Kind regards!

entry.title: Re: The Delivery Channel for Software Logistics Tools: "Software Logistics Toolset 1.0"
entry.content: Youtube video on SL Toolset is private and not visible. Can you make public?

entry.title: Re: tp - DB connect: link to database failed
entry.content: Sorry but how can I answer to your post? Not "comment" but answer to also attach screenshots?

entry.title: Re: Account is currently blocked by user RFC_ALE during IDOC creation
entry.content: A lock error "by user XXXX" means that the user XXXX is working in parallel on the same object. That's it. It depends on the exact case. It's impossible to answer without information.

entry.title: Re: tp - DB connect: link to database failed
entry.content: Relevant to note 1886616 I see the following in environment variables and in default.pfl - database is Oracle here:

entry.title: Re: How Can I Use the @sap/cds-rfc Plugin?
entry.content: @gregorw made a demo repo about this, see the config here: https://github.com/gregorwolf/cap-rfc/blob/21c2a45d82ca5dc6d0fdd45954705b76b4d3d1fd/.npmrc#L2

entry.title: Re: SAP Certification Transformation: How You Can Get and Stay Certified
entry.content:
Dear @kamala13,

when taken the certification in 2021 it will expire on 31.03.2025. To ensure that you keep certified you can please setup your SAP Universal ID ( Watch this video for setup guidance) and sign in to your My Learning page and go to "Certifications" to see your current certifications and expiration dates.   If your certification does not appear there then it might require you to take an alternative certification.

Please in this case check find and complete an alternative certification exam by March 31, 2025, to maintain your certified status.

Kind Regards, Suzanne

entry.title: Re: tp - DB connect: link to database failed
entry.content: And I can't locate a trans.log file to see the contents as mentioned in the note.

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP)
entry.content: Thank you , how can I start a project for learning on vs code after installing all these extensions ?

entry.title: Re: Percentage in cross model
entry.content:
I assume there might be few options: 

  1. Use cross model copy to copy the percentage from source(human resource) model to target(expense) model, and add a step in the end to delete the copied percentage. Note that, cross model copy can take over calulated measure in the source model. 
  2. Directly calculate percentage from source model by LINK keyword. 
  3. If this tenant is ON HANA Cloud, calculate measure result can be used direct with addtional statement CONFIG.READ_CALCULATED_MEMBER_VALUES  on top

entry.title: Re: SAP B1 profit & loss statement has recorded a cost tr...
entry.content:
Unfortunately none of the responses below have provided a resolution.  I've included screenshots of both the P&L and Trial balance

P&L which shows the YTD balance as GBP4,450.34:

Trial Balance  which shows the closing balance of GBP4,432.78:

The Account Balance shows the transactions that took place during the month:

I would like to keep this question marked as unresolved so that maybe if someone else experiences this then they can add to this

entry.title: Re: Abap2xlsx: print internal table without header

entry.title: Re: Abap2xlsx: print internal table without header

entry.title: Re: During our recent physical verification, we identified some excess assets that were not part of
entry.content:
You should provide some SAP managed tags to help identify your system type and version (ECC, S/4HANA cloud public, etc.) and the company country,

Else it's hard to answer from transaction AS91 to Fiori App F7280 ...

entry.title: Re: The secret to better "looking" tasks in SAP Build Process Automation: Customizing My I
entry.content: Perfect, thank you!

entry.title: How to use Excel Add-in for SAC for public Dimension with read & write property ?
entry.content:
My requirement is to open a model's public dimension including read and write properties on excel. Is there a way to download this list fully and continue working on excel?  Currently i am doing this by copy-paste operation. But i would like to automate the process by calling from excel addin so that i dont have to navigate myself to public dimension of the model's page everytime.

When i try to call the model from excel it does not show me the read, write property which i need in my requirement.

Does anyone have a similiar experience or an idea on how to solve?

Thanks in advance!

SAP Analytics Cloud #SAC Addin for Excel

entry.title: Re: 401 Unauthorized - Build Apps Unable to Access BTP De...
entry.content:
IMPORTANT: I think what confused me is that you were connecting to SPA the "old way" and the documentation you referenced, though still valid, is somewhat superseded by the "new" way: https://help.sap.com/docs/build-apps/service-guide/processes

There are new flow functions that make connecting much easier and less error prone.

entry.title: Re: Total Compensation Individual Guideline Percentage de...
entry.content:
@xavierlegarrec I found out what format is controllihg the Individual guideline percentage: it's this field: 

So I changed this and now its working correctly for me as well 🙂

entry.title: Automatic Goods Receipts for P.O : something new ?
entry.content:
Hi experts,

Several topics, dating back a few years, have addressed the automation of goods receipts for purchase orders in SAP S4 HANA  : it did not seem possible with standard settings, but with specific development (thanks, in particular, to the use of BAPI_GOODSMVT_CREATE).

Looking for a potential enhancement of the standard, I have researched the current documentation without success. The automation of goods receipts for purchase orders still does not seem possible as standard.

I might have missed something in my research. Has anyone found a solution, please?

Thank you,

Erwan

entry.title: Re: how to create a file excel .xlsx
entry.content: Without Abap2XLSX or OLE2, you could use same tools than ALV grid export, look for class cl_salv_bs_tt_util, or play with transformations.

entry.title: How to check change logs in table CDHDR and or Utilizing CDHDR and CDPOS SAP Tables for Change Logs
entry.content:
How to check change logs in table CDHDR and CDPOS

CDHDR: Records the header information for document changes.
CDPOS: Stores the item-level details of the changes

Utilizing CDHDR and CDPOS SAP Tables for Change Logs or How to check change logs in table CDHDR and CDPOS

I am going to show you that how can we check the changes logs by using table CDHDR and CDPOS

Go to Transaction Code SE16N.

To view any table in SAP, you can go to transaction code SE16 or SE16N. Below is a screenshot of SE16N where I have entered the CDHDR table.

 

  1. Narrow down or filter your search results by filling in the parameters for Table CDHDR

We start with table CDHDR because we need to note the “change document number” of the document we are analyzing. The “change document number” will be our linkage / basis of search for Table CDPOS.

Click on the Execute Button to proceed. For now, you can review the CDHDR results.

Notice the object value is a concatenation of the SAP Accounting Document details (Client + Company Code + Document Number + Fiscal Year) we are concerned with. That is one way to determine the change document number.

 

You can also refer to the Object Change field to see what kind of change was done.

  1. Copy all concerned “change document numbers” from CDHDR table and open a separate window for SE16N CDPOS search.

 

You need to copy the “change document numbers” (red box) so you can paste them as part of the selection criteria in Table CDPOS. We open a separate table so that we still have the CDHDR table view.

  1. Narrow down or filter your search results by filling in the parameters for Table CDPOS.

Here you will paste the “change document numbers” you have copied from CDHDR. This should establish the linkage and show you the line item details of the change documents.

Click on the Execute Button to proceed.

  1. Proceed with your analysis or extract.

Now that you have the line items of the change document, notice that there are “before” (Old Value) and “after” (New Value) details of the changes. This should give you a good overview of the changes done.

Rewards if you found this helpfull.

Regards,

Lalit Nagar

entry.title: Re: How do I fill DMEE tag ReqdExctnDt/DtTm
entry.content: Thank you, I will have a look at this with our developer

entry.title: Re: How to select combinable roadmaps
entry.content: Did you download the "For Creation Using Template" option? Using this you can delete all entries in the download and then cut and paste the new tasks which will then be added to the project in CALM. Its also very sensitive to the right combination of statuses etc so you can't create new tasks by manually adding into the creation template and show the source as Roadmap Content. The source would need to be something like "Manually Created" and the type could be "Roadmap Task"

entry.title: Re: Connect to DataBaseCompany(B1DI) failed during testing B1DI connection in Integration Framework
entry.content: Thank you!

entry.title: Re: Show overview about security policies (SECPOL)
entry.content:
Can I ask you if we could export the settings we have defined for SECPOL to a change request, so that we can use it in ABAP system/clients desired ?

 

Regards, Sebastian

entry.title: Transform Your ERP and BW Journey with Our Expert-Led Series
entry.content:
Are you ready to take your ERP and BW transformation to the next level? We’re excited to introduce a series of pre-recorded webcasts designed guide you through the complexities and opportunities of evolving your SAP landscape using SAP Enterprise Support.

Presented in partnership with the UK & Ireland SAP User Group (UKISUG), this e-learning is tailored to SAP customers who are embarking on or progressing through their transformation journeys. Whether you’re just starting or well underway, these sessions will equip you with the knowledge and strategies you need to succeed.

What You'll Gain

By watching these recordings, you’ll:

  • Explore SAP’s essential transformation tools, learning content and services to support your organization’s unique needs.
  • Gain expert insights into critical topics to modernize your systems effectively.
  • Prepare for the future by learning how to avoid common challenges and optimize your investments.

Recording Topics

Here’s a look at the sessions available in this series:

  1. SAP Enterprise Support: Learn what's offered, what's new, and why you should engage.
  2. Maximize your investment with SAP Enterprise Support Academy and SAP Enterprise Support value maps.
  3. The Cost of Inaction: How software obsolescence increases your SAP system's technical debt.
  4. Unlocking the Future: Transform your ECC System to SAP S/4HANA using SAP Enterprise Support.
  5. Safeguard your SAP BW modernization with SAP Enterprise Support.
  6. Maximizing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Benefits with SAP Enterprise Support: Navigating the Transition and Gaining Valuable Assistance.

Start with the First Session

Begin your learning journey with “SAP Enterprise Support: Learn what’s offered, what’s new, and why you should engage.” This session provides an in-depth look at SAP Enterprise Support, our offerings, and how we help organizations like yours achieve transformation success:

Access Recordings

But that’s not all - our commitment to your success goes beyond these recordings. For ongoing support and actionable guidance tailored to your unique SAP journey, we invite you to explore SAP Enterprise Support Value Maps.

Get Moving with SAP Enterprise Support Value Maps

Included with every SAP cloud subscription, value maps offer structured, product-level Learning Rooms where you can:

  • Access curated content and self-service platforms.
  • Engage with fellow customers and SAP experts.
  • Discover continuous quality checks for SAP S/4HANA Cloud in the respective section of the value maps.

Access Value Maps in 3 Easy Steps:

  1. Sign up for SAP Learning Hub, edition for SAP Enterprise Support (please allow 2 hours for processing).
  2. Access value maps via the Learning Hub.
  3. Navigate to the SAP Enterprise Support value maps for SAP S/4HANA Cloud. Once in the value maps, you can collaborate in forums, ask questions, or “Request a Call” to speak with one of our experts for further assistance.

Value maps are intuitive to navigate, but we recommend scheduling an onboarding call to tailor the experience to your specific needs and highlight the most relevant content.

Got a question about value maps or our content?

  • Use the “ask questions” feature in the value maps.
  • Or, contact us at: sapesvaluemaps@sap.com

Brought to you by the SAP Enterprise Support value map team for SAP S/4HANA

entry.title: SAP Customer Data Cloud Certification Update and Stay Certified Release
entry.content:
On behalf of the SAP CRM & CX Product Learning CoE team, I am thrilled to announce the official release of the updated SAP Customer Data Cloud Certification and the new SAP Customer Data Cloud Stay Certified release.

SAP Certified Associate - Implementation Consultant - SAP Customer Data Cloud

This certification verifies that you possess the core skills required for the development consultant profile and proves that you have an overall understanding and in-depth technical skills to participate as a member of an SAP Customer Data Cloud project team in a mentored role. The certification is recommended as an entry-level qualification.

It is important to note that proper preparation for the exam is highly recommended. To ensure success, consider attending the following:

  • C4H62 SAP Customer Data Cloud Implementation Training: This course contains ALL the essential content required for passing the exam. Enrolling in this course is highly beneficial.
  • Browse SAP Learning site to find available SAP Customer Data Cloud Learning Journeys, Videos, Hands-on Practice Systems and Live Sessions.

 

Stay Certified

A Stay Certified program ensures you aren't left behind as SAP Customer Data Cloud technology advances. It provides you the opportunity to familiarize yourself with the latest updates and product changes and keep your certification valid. The Stay Certified program contains:

  • Stay Certified SAP Customer Data Cloud: An online course where you can learn about the major enhancements for certification SAP Customer Data Cloud to prepare for the assessment to stay certified.
  • An assessment which checks your knowledge on the latest features. When you feel comfortable with the material, you can take the assessment. If you pass it successfully, your certification will be valid for one year.

Note: The Stay Certified offer is for subscribed learners on learning.sap.com platform.

entry.title: Talent Intelligent Hub Migration - Impact to PMGM template with custom competency section
entry.content:
Hi,

When we migrate to TIH from CoC, will the competency ids change? Wanted to understand if there will an impact to existing PMGM forms which has custom competency section. Should the competencies be again mapped to the PMGM template via new competency ids (from TIH) in provisioning?

Thanks,

Neethu Raghavan

entry.title: Re: Parallel operations in IBP S&OP TS Based Heuristics
entry.content:
Hi,
your scenario should work with a production source that has a lead time of 4 weeks and resource consumptions with according Capacity Consumption Offsets.
See Production Lead Time, section Capacity Consumption Offsets for more information.
Cheers, Carsten

Best regards,

Eric F.

entry.title: Query : manage the several year a nd period for the initial budget
entry.content:
Hi,

 We want to create the query with 100 projects with several years selected for the initial budget

We want to compare the initial budget with the current budget

The year and period for the initial budget depends on when the project started

 Example query with3 projects

 Prompt : Year :2025 Period : 001

 Option 1: use the range for year and period for the initial budget      

 

Initial budget

Current budget

Variance

 

Year

period

Amount

Year

period

Amount

 

Customizing

[2000;2024]

[001;012]

 

Prompt:2025

Prompt:001

 

 

Project 1

2022

005

100

2025

001

95

5

Project 2

2012

004

200

2025

001

210

-10

Project 3

2024

012

50

2025

001

48

2

Inconvenient: bad performance because the range is large for year and period

 Option 2: transformation the year and period for the initial budget with the fixed value

 

Initial budget

Current budget

Variance

 

Year

period

Amount

Year

period

Amount

 

Customizing

9999

012

 

Prompt:2025

Prompt:001

 

 

Project 1

2022 9999

005 012

100

2025

001

95

5

Project 2

2012 9999

004 012

200

2025

001

210

-10

Project 3

2024 9999

012 012

50

2025

001

48

2

Inconvenient : lost information when the project started

Question : do you have the other option? 

Thanks 

Bastien

Without variant :

Parameter 1TBD
Parameter 2TBD
Parameter 3TBD

entry.title: Re: Difference in the value of the condition associated with the withholding tax between SD/FI docum
entry.content: Share your pricing procedure configuration

entry.title: SAP Cloud ALM Integration for SAP Convergent Mediation by DigitalRoute
entry.content:
For organizations utilizing SAP Convergent Mediation within the SAP Billing and Revenue Innovation Management (SAP BRIM) project, a frequently asked question is how to enable technical monitoring for SAP Convergent Mediation.

If your organization is asking the same question, you will be pleased to know that a new SAP Knowledge Base Article (SAP KBA) has been published in December 2024 to address the need for technical monitoring for SAP Convergent Mediation using SAP Cloud ALM.

SAP KBA 3551398 provides the integration guide document that describes the configuration steps required in both SAP Cloud ALM and SAP Convergent Mediation. Additionally, it includes a sample configuration export for SAP Convergent Mediation that can be imported into an existing SAP Convergent Mediation installation with release 9.3.

Please note that this integration is made possible through the use of out-of-the box features in SAP Convergent Mediation release 9.3 in combination with the sample workflow configuration provided in the KBA. The sample workflow configuration is not within the scope of standard SAP Convergent Mediation product support.

I would encourage you to explore this integration pattern with your project team, and do reach out to your SAP or DigitalRoute contact if you require professional assistance.

Hope you find this useful!

entry.title: Food Recovery Program simplified in SAP HCM Payroll
entry.content:
Introduction:

Organizations across the globe, provide discounted food benefits to their employees. Discounted food rates are provided, and employees must pay minimum for Breakfast, Lunch & Dinner in the organization. It is important to capture the food rates of the employees in HCM based on their consumption and deduct them from their salary.

Scenario:

Food rates across locations can vary depending on the cost of living with employer and vendor charges.

Example:

Simple Approach:

Simple approach to this data to be loaded in the system is creating wage types in IT0015 and updating the rates with respect to every location.

This is a very general configuration but requires lot of manual effort and time consuming and its very complex as food rates can change every month leading to errors as per the scenario below.

Changing food rates monthly:

Example above we can see that varying food rates become a tedious activity for the end user to update and prone to errors and wastage.

Preferred Approach to this case:

This can be managed through a program more effectively and efficiently reducing the burden and workload of end users.

We can come up with a program similar to the example below:

The format above should be created along with functional specific document with source of data upload table IT0015. This is sent to ABAP developer of your organization post approval.

Post which the developer can create 2 programs:

Program 1: ZHR_Foodrates:

This is designed to update the food rates across locations based on effective dates during rate changes.

Above example clearly tells location 1000(India) with Sublocation 1010(Manesar) requires the food rates for Group A1(Managers).

BK – Breakfast, LU-Lunch, SN-Snacks.

This will offer flexibility to load the changing food rates in the system without any hassle.

Program 2: ZHR_EREMUN_UPL

Note: Program names can vary depending on the organization and ABAP developer name preference.

Template for upload:

Employee code, date and number of times food availed in the month are saved in an excel file and processed to upload every month.

Program Execution:

Conclusion:

Food recovery is a very essential feature in SAP every organization will have to implement at some stage. The above steps make the end user experience smooth and client-oriented approach which will be appreciated by clients across the globe.

entry.title: Re: Integrating gCTS with Transport Organizer processes
entry.content:
Hi Karin,

We are considering implementing GCTS in our project. Currently, I am experimenting with the Registry for Customizing and performing some proof-of-concept (POC) testing using two branches, each corresponding to a different client (mandant). During testing, I encountered a few issues and would appreciate your advice:

  1. Registry and Object Conflicts:
    Can we add the same object to the registry in different repositories or branches? My goal is to import the same customizing into different repositories or branches. However, I encounter an error stating, "Object could not be registered because of conflicts."

  2. Task Release Issues:
    I am experiencing problems with releasing tasks when BRANCH_HANDLING_ENABLE is active and the transport attribute GIT_BRANCH is filled. It seems that, to import objects into a branch, the branch must first be active. However, even then, the logic does not allow the task to be released, and an error "Repository branch handling failed for ... ) is displayed. It appears that the issue arises in the method zcl_im_gcts_badi->handle_branch_operation.

  3. Custom Prefix Object Removal:
    Objects with a custom prefix (beginning with “/”) cannot be removed from the registry. An error message stating, "Object could not be unregistered => Invalid URI segment," is displayed.

Could you please advice?

Thank you in advance for your help!

Best regards,

entry.title: Re: block outbound delivery creation based on cofirmed de...

entry.title: MILESTONE BILLING IN SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
Hello Readers,

Hope you all are doing Great!

Today we will talk about the Milestone billing used in SAP S/4 HANA. The blog will give you a crisp and clear understanding of the Milestone billing.

Milestone billing means distributing the total amount to be billed over multiple billing dates in the billing plan. This approach is particularly beneficial for industries such as engineering, construction & professional services where projects are linked to project completion phases.

Business example:

Engineering firm such as Windmill Manufacturing company uses milestone billing as follow,

  1. Contract Phase - 30%
  1. Design Phase - 40%
  2. Assembly Phase - 20%
  3. Final Invoice Delivery Phase -10 %

 At each milestone Billing date Invoice triggers to the Client.

Below are the Configuration steps for Milestone Billing in SAP S/4HANA:

  1. We need to Define Billing Plan Type in SPRO/ Transaction Code: OVBO.
  2. Define Date Description in SPRO/ Transaction code: OVB0: As per Business requirement create Date description such as- Contract phase, Design phase, Assembly phase, Final Invoice phase.
  3. Define and Assign Date Categories: Billing rule- (Depends on Business requirement of percentage Basis or Value base etc.)
  4. Assign Date category for Billing Plan.
  5. Maintain Date proposals for billing plan type: Maintaining the Billing Date, Billing Rule, Date Description and Billing Type Information will be repeated automatically in upcoming milestone orders created in the system (As per client requirement).
  6. Assign Billing plan Types to Sales Document Types in SPRO/ Transaction Code: VOFA.
  7. Assign Billing Plan type to Item Categories.
  8. In some Business Cases Milestone comes from PS Module:
    1. Create Milestone Billing in Project Systems (PS) Module Use Transaction Code: CJ20N (Project Builder)
    2. Define milestone under project activities and specify milestone dates.
    3. Link each milestone to a billing plan date.
    4. Once the Milestone is confirmed by the Project system team, the billing block is removed and billing can be done accordingly.
  9. Create Sales order: Go to Billing plan tab in Item level & adjust the billing dates and amounts according to milestone and project progress.
  10. Now system is ready to create Invoices based on Milestone.

Note: To Add or Remove Billing Plan Tab in Sales order - Header level Screen > Go to Transaction code VOV8 Select sales document type in Billing Tab > Billing Plan type maintain as Business requirements.

Advantages of Milestone billing to clients:

Milestone billing gives clients the option to change project focuses while reducing the financial risk by connecting payments to finished milestones. It helps businesses keep high quality, Standard and builds a trust in client. Linking payments to the project phase clears the result encourages trust and openness, Clients can monitor the progress and make sure payments match the billing value delivered. This approach also improves project management by setting clear targets and timelines, which helps stay on schedule and budget while matching cash flow Alignment with project advancement.

Upgrade in S/4HANA compared to ECC:

  1. User Interface: Fiori application provides modern & easy to use interface, easy to track and manage.
  2. Milestone Achievement rates are progressed due to embedded analytical dashboards and by allowing real- time Tracking.
  3. Integration with SAP Project Systems (PS) updates billing milestones by project status, making it more accurate and cutting down on manual tasks.
  4. The integration of real-time connectivity with the Universal Journal facilitates instantaneous modifications to milestone billing financial documentation, thereby obviating the necessity for routine verifications.
  5. The system provides quicker processing of big billing data and allows for real-time checks for billing results.

Conclusion: Milestone billing is a useful way to send invoices that brings several advantages to companies. These advantages include better cash flow control, improved project handling, and stronger relationships with clients. By linking payments to certain project milestones, companies can maintain financial steadiness, lower financial risk, and encourage quality work. Using milestone billing helps companies succeed and gain both profit and customer satisfaction.

entry.title: Implementing Search Help Exit for Authorized Data Access
entry.content:
In the world of SAP, Search Helps play a crucial role in simplifying user input by providing dropdown lists or search options directly in the application. However, in certain scenarios, it is essential to control the visibility of these options to ensure that only authorized users can access specific data. This is where the Search Help Exit comes into play. 

A Search Help Exit is a powerful mechanism that allows developers to customize and enhance the standard behavior of Search Helps in SAP. By leveraging a Search Help Exit, you can filter the data dynamically at runtime based on custom logic, ensuring that only specific users or groups of users have access to the relevant data. 

In this blog, we’ll walk you through a practical example of implementing a Search Help Exit to restrict data visibility to authorized users. We'll cover the following: 

  1. Understanding Search Help Exits: 
  1. What is a Search Help Exit? 
  1. Key steps in the Search Help process (SELONE, PRESEL, SELECT, DISP, RETURN). 
  1. Creating a Custom Authorization Table: 
  1. How to store user-specific authorizations in a custom SAP table. 
  1. Developing a Custom Search Help Exit: 
  1. Step-by-step guide to implement a Search Help Exit with ABAP code. 
  1. Filtering data dynamically based on authorized users. 
  1. Assigning and Testing the Exit: 
  1. Linking the Search Help Exit to your Search Help in SE11. 
  1. Verifying the behavior with authorized and unauthorized users. 

By the end of this blog, you’ll have a clear understanding of how to enhance the functionality of Search Helps to align with your business requirements, improving both data security and user experience. Whether you’re an SAP ABAP developer or a functional consultant, this guide will equip you with the tools to implement Search Help Exits effectively. 

So, let’s dive into the world of Search Help Exits and learn how to restrict data to authorized users! 

As we know Search Help Exit is a Function Module that has a Predefined Interface , which is used to write the logic for Search help to Enhance the Search help. 

The Standard Function Module  for Search help is F4IF_SHLP_EXIT_EXAMPLE, So we make a copy of this search help exit and create our own search help exit. 

So we have parameters Inside this Function Module like Changing parameter [ SHLP AND CALL CONTROL] and Table parameter like [ SHLP_TAB  AND RECORD_TAB] . 

So First we need to create a Custom Database Table , having 2 fields MANDT AND UNAME, 

And whatever the user name which we provide inside the UNAME , only that users will be authorized and all other users are unauthorized, only the names provided in the table , only that users will be able to see the data , apart from those users no other users will be able to see the data. 

So we have a parameter called as CALL CONTROL , Inside this parameter we have various options like 

SELONE, PRESEL, SELECT, DISP, RETURN 

SELONE  -  It is used to select one of the elementary search helps, This step is applicable for collective search help. 

PRESEL – Enter Selection Conditions. 

SELECT – Select values. 

DISP – Display values. 

RETURN –  To return the values. 

 

so here I have maintained 1 user [ HANAUSER28] so only that user will be having the access to display the data, other user will not have the access to display the data. 

 

Now create a Search help  

 

Then create a Search help exit , and In the source code write the below Code . 

 

 DATA: LV_USER TYPE CHAR30. 
 
  IF CALLCONTROL-STEP = 'DISP'. 
    SELECT SINGLE UNAME 
    FROM ZUSER_AUTHOR 
      INTO LV_USER 
      WHERE UNAME =  SY-UNAME. 
 
    IF SY-SUBRC <> 0. 
      REFRESH : RECORD_TAB. 
    ENDIF. 
  ENDIF.

 

Put a break point  inside the Function module , and Run the search help using the Authorized User . 

 

So Initially the CALLCONTROL-STEP will be SELONE, Then click on F8. 

Then it will be PRESEL1 , click on f8,  

Then it will be PRESEL,  

Then it will be SELECT  

Again click on f8 , value will be DISP 

 

AGAIN CLICK ON F8, The values will be displayed. 

 

Now Try to Login with another user Id. 

 

Now if you are trying to run the search help, It will not display the value.

entry.title: SAP Business Technology Platform: “Change Agent” or “Scale Agent” Part 6 – How to select SAP BTP
entry.content:
How to decide when to use what services in SAP BTP

 

Below are the main SAP BTP archetypes:

  • Application Modernization
  • Data Modernization (Data with value)
  • AI & Gen AI

 

First, identify whether the application is transactional or analytical. Transactional applications fall under App Modernization, while analytical ones belong to Data Modernization. This is the initial step in building an application using SAP BTP. Further enhancements can be made through AI or Generative AI, introducing the AI & Generative AI archetype. Refer to Figure-1 for a broad classification of applications.

 

 

Figure 1 – Determining application as Transactional or Analytical

 

Once the correct use-case type is identified, we can begin defining its architecture. This helps in determining the essential SAP BTP services required for the use case..

 

Application Modernization archetype

Exploring the Application Modernization Archetype, the key services involved include:

  • Integration service
  • Application runtime
  • Business Process Automation
  • Mobile services
  • Workzone
  • Identity services

 

Figure-2 outlines the decision flow, highlighting questions that classify the application type and identify the services forming the solution architecture.

 

Figure 2 – Application Modernization archetype decision matrix

 

Step 1 – Low-code or No-code Tool

First, determine if the application is simple which does not require extensive programming knowledge. Here are some examples of such applications:

 

Application Type

Finance

Sales, Service & Marketing

Procurement & Expense Management

Supply Chain

Human Resources

Operations

IT

Data collection Apps

X

X

X

X

X

X

X

Performance Evaluation

 

 

 

 

X

 

 

Dashboard & reporting tools

X

X

X

X

X

X

X

Customer relationship

 

X

 

 

 

 

 

Employee On-boarding

 

 

 

 

X

 

X

Approval Workflows

X

X

X

X

X

X

X

Project Monitoring

 

 

 

 

 

X

 

Project Task Assignment

 

 

 

 

 

X

 

Project Progress Tracking

 

 

 

 

 

X

 

Inventory Ordering

 

 

 

X

 

 

 

Inventory Tracking

 

 

 

X

 

 

 

Event Management - Registrations

 

X

 

 

 

 

 

Employee Training

 

 

 

 

X

 

 

Compliance Applications

X

 

 

 

 

X

 

 

SAP BTP (Business Technology Platform) Build offers a suite of tools and services that allows developers and business users to create applications with little to no coding. Here are several types of applications that can be built using SAP BTP Build's no-code capabilities:

 

  1. **Business Process Applications**: Automate and optimize various business processes such as approvals, order processing, and incident management. These applications often involve data entry forms, workflow automation, and integration with backend systems.

 

  1. **Data Collection and Reporting Tools**: Create applications for collecting data from various sources and generating reports. These applications can be used for surveys, audits, and other forms of data gathering.

 

  1. **Dashboards and Analytics**: Develop interactive dashboards to visualize data from different business units. These applications typically integrate with data sources to provide real-time analytics and insights.

 

  1. **Customer and Employee Self-Service Portals**: Build portals that allow customers or employees to access information, perform tasks like submitting requests, or manage their profiles without needing to directly contact support or HR.

 

  1. **Task Management Systems**: Create systems to help teams manage tasks, track progress, and collaborate effectively. These can include features like task assignment, priority setting, and deadlines.

 

  1. **Mobile Applications**: Design mobile-friendly applications for various business needs, which can be used by employees in the field or by customers.

 

  1. **Form-based Applications**: Develop applications to replace paper forms with digital versions, simplifying data entry and processing workflows.

 

  1. **Integration Interfaces**: Set up simple integrations between different applications and services, allowing data to be shared across platforms without complex development work.

 

  1. **Productivity Tools**: Develop tools to enhance productivity, such as custom calculators, converters, or document generators.

 

SAP BTP Build's no-code environment is particularly beneficial for enabling non-technical users to participate in application development, fostering innovation and agility within organizations.

 

These examples highlight the versatility of SAP Build in developing tailored applications to meet various business needs, enhancing operational efficiency, and simplifying routine tasks like workflow or approval applications. Utilize SAP Build, a no-code tool that allows fast application development without coding for such scenarios.

 

Step 2 - Automation

Next, assess if it's a repetitive task that can be automated, where the requirement is to streamline and automate business processes. It can combine process management, process automation, decision management, and workflow management. Here are some types of applications and processes that can be built and automated using SAP Build Process Automation:

 

Application Type

Finance

Sales, Service & Marketing

Procurement & Expense Management

Supply Chain

Human Resources

Operations

IT

Sales Order processing - intake & validation

X

X

 

X

 

X

 

Purchase Order processing - intake & validation

X

 

X

 

 

X

 

Invoice Processing - Capture, validation & Payment

X

 

X

 

 

 

 

Employee On-boarding - Document Collection, access Provisioning, Training Assignments, new employee profile

 

 

 

 

X

 

 

Customer Service – Enquiries, Generating Tickets, Request routing, Generating responses

X

X

X

 

X

 

X

Expense Management - Report Submission, approvals, reimbursements

 

 

X

 

 

 

 

Supplychain - Order fulfilment

 

 

 

X

 

 

 

Supplychain - Supplier coordination

 

 

 

X

 

 

 

Compliance checks

X

 

 

 

 

X

X

Audit Trails

X

X

X

 

X

 

X

Human Resources - Leave Request , Performance Evaluations, off-boarding procedures

 

 

 

 

X

 

 

Sales - Campaign tracking, Lead Management

 

X

 

 

 

 

 

Financial Reporting & Reconciliation

X

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

SAP BTP Build Process Automation is a powerful tool that enables businesses to automate and optimize their processes. Here are some types of applications and solutions that can be built using this platform:

 

  1. **Invoice Processing Automation**: Streamline accounts payable by automating the extraction, validation, and approval of invoices. This reduces manual errors and speeds up processing times.

 

  1. **Employee Onboarding**: Automate the employee onboarding process by integrating HR systems to handle document submissions, identity verification, and access provisioning.

 

  1. **Sales Order Processing**: Optimize the sales order workflow by automating order entry, approval, and fulfillment, ensuring quicker response times and improving customer satisfaction.

 

  1. **Expense Management**: Automate the submission, approval, and reimbursement of employee expenses to enhance efficiency and reduce the administrative burden.

 

  1. **Customer Support**: Implement automated customer support bots that can handle common queries, escalate issues to human agents, and integrate seamlessly with CRM systems.

 

  1. **Procurement and Supplier Management**: Automate procurement workflows, including request-for-quote processes, supplier approvals, and order tracking to enhance procurement efficiency.

 

  1. **Compliance and Audit Management**: Build applications that automate compliance checks and audit processes to ensure regulatory compliance and minimize risk.

 

  1. **Data Migration and Integration**: Automate data migration tasks across SAP and non-SAP systems, ensuring seamless integration and synchronization of data.

 

  1. **Inventory Management**: Implement automated inventory management solutions to track stock levels, manage reorder points, and optimize supply chain operations.

 

  1. **Facility Management**: Automate maintenance requests, scheduling, and resource allocation for facility management operations.

 

These applications leverage the capabilities of SAP BTP Build Process Automation to enhance efficiency, accuracy, and speed across various business functions.

 

These applications and processes can significantly enhance operational efficiency, reduce manual errors, and provide real-time insights into business operations. SAP Build Process Automation comes with a variety of pre-built content and templates that can be customized to fit specific organizational needs, making it a flexible solution for businesses aiming to optimize their workflows.

 

Step 3 – Workzone or Portal Service

Analyze the user base—whether internal or external. For internal users, employ SAP Workzone; for external users, use the portal service.

SAP Work Zone and SAP Portal Service are components of the SAP Business Technology Platform that facilitate the creation of engaging, intuitive digital workplace and portal experiences. Here are some types of applications and use cases that can be built using these services:

 

Application Type

Finance

Sales, Service & Marketing

Procurement & Expense Management

Supply Chain

Human Resources

Operations

IT

Employee Self Service Portals

X

 

X

 

X

 

X

Customer Portals

 

X

 

 

 

 

 

Supplier Portals

 

 

X

X

 

 

 

Project Collaboration workspaces

X

X

X

X

X

X

X

Knowledge Management systems

X

X

X

X

X

X

X

On-boarding and training portals

 

 

 

 

X

 

 

Corporate Intranets

 

 

 

 

 

X

X

Service Request and Ticketing Systems

 

X

 

 

 

 

 

Integrated Business Dashboards

X

X

X

X

X

X

X

Training and Development Applications

X

X

 

 

X

 

X

 

SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) Work Zone and Portal services are designed to help organizations build and deploy various types of applications and digital experiences that enhance user productivity and engagement. Here are some types of applications you can build using these services:

 

  1. **Employee Portals:** Centralized platforms where employees can access corporate information, documents, applications, and services in one place. These portals facilitate communication and collaboration within the organization.

 

  1. **Supplier Portals:** Platforms that enable efficient collaboration and communication with suppliers, providing them access to relevant data, documents, and processes needed for effective supply chain management.

 

  1. **Customer Portals:** Allowing customers to access personalized information, services, and support. These portals can enhance customer experience by providing self-service options and personalized content.

 

  1. **Self-Service Applications:** Applications that allow users to manage their profiles, submit requests, and access self-help information. This can range from HR-related services to IT support and beyond.

 

  1. **Dashboard and Analytics Applications:** Integrate with various data sources to provide users with visual insights and reporting capabilities. These applications help in making informed decisions by presenting data in an easily interpretable manner.

 

  1. **Mobile Apps:** Utilizing responsive design capabilities to create mobile-friendly applications that can be accessed on various devices, enhancing mobility and access to important business processes and information on the go.

 

  1. **Collaboration Platforms:** Applications that facilitate collaboration and social interaction among users, integrating features like chat, file sharing, forums, and community spaces.

 

  1. **Custom Business Applications:** Tailored applications that meet specific business requirements, leveraging SAP BTP's capabilities to integrate with other SAP and third-party systems to streamline business processes.

 

  1. **Training and Development Platforms:** Systems that provide learning management capabilities, allowing employees to access training materials, webinars, and courses for skill development and certification.

 

These applications leverage the tools and functionalities provided by SAP BTP Work Zone and Portal services to create comprehensive, user-friendly, and efficient digital experiences.

 

By leveraging these services, organizations can improve user engagement, streamline operations, and enhance productivity by providing tailored experiences to employees, customers, and partners.

 

Step 4 – Mobile Services

Check if the application needs remote or mobile access. If needed, develop mobile applications using Mobile Services.

SAP Mobile Services provides a versatile platform for developing a variety of mobile applications. Here are some types of applications that can be built using SAP Mobile Services:

 

Application Type

Finance

Sales, Service & Marketing

Procurement & Expense Management

Supply Chain

Human Resources

Operations

IT

Customer Relationship - Interactions, campaigns, service requests

 

X

 

 

 

 

 

Field Service Apps - job details, work status, service reports

 

 

 

X

 

X

 

Supply chain - Warehouse management, inventory, logistics

 

 

 

X

 

 

 

Human Resources - Employee Self-Service, leave requests, time tracking, benefits & performance evaluation

 

 

 

 

X

 

 

Business Intelligence Apps

X

X

X

X

X

X

X

e-Commerce and Retail apps

 

X

 

X

 

 

 

Workflow & Approval Apps

X

X

X

X

X

X

X

Collaboration & Communication Apps

X

X

X

X

X

X

X

IOT enabled Applications

 

 

 

 

 

X

 

 

 

SAP BTP Mobile Services provide a comprehensive range of tools and services that facilitate the development, deployment, and management of mobile applications. Here are some types of applications that can be built using SAP BTP Mobile Services:

 

  1. **Enterprise Mobility Applications**:

   - Applications that allow employees to access enterprise data and perform tasks on the go. Examples include HR apps, analytics dashboards, and CRM tools.

 

  1. **S/4HANA Mobile Applications**:

   - Extensions or interfaces for SAP S/4HANA that provide mobile access to critical business processes and data.

 

  1. **Field Service Management Apps**:

   - Applications designed for field technicians to receive work orders, capture job details, and update service status directly from their mobile devices.

 

  1. **Supply Chain and Logistics Apps**:

   - Solutions for inventory management, tracking shipments, warehouse management, and logistics to enhance supply chain operations.

 

  1. **Retail and E-commerce Apps**:

   - Mobile applications for enhancing customer engagement through features like product catalogs, personalized recommendations, and mobile payment options.

 

  1. **Mobile Asset Management**:

   - Applications that allow for real-time tracking and management of assets, maintenance scheduling, and condition monitoring.

 

  1. **Employee Self-Service Apps**:

   - Applications that allow employees to manage their profiles, request leaves, view pay slips, and perform other HR-related tasks from their mobile devices.

 

  1. **Customer Engagement and Experience Apps**:

   - Apps designed to enhance customer interaction and service, such as loyalty programs, feedback collection, and personalized marketing.

 

  1. **IoT-Enabled Applications**:

   - Integrations with Internet of Things devices to provide mobile interfaces for monitoring and controlling IoT-connected equipment and sensors.

 

  1. **Healthcare and Wellness Apps**:

    - Solutions for patient management, appointment scheduling, telemedicine, and health monitoring.

 

SAP Mobile Services supports a wide range of devices and operating systems, and it integrates seamlessly with SAP’s backend systems, ensuring secure and efficient data handling and syncing. The platform also includes tools for mobile app lifecycle management, from development and deployment to monitoring and analysis, providing a comprehensive solution for enterprise mobile app development.

 

Step 5 – ABAP on Cloud

Determine if the application demands tight integration with extensive ERP data processing. In this case, use ABAP Cloud or on-stack solutions.

 

The decision to develop ABAP on the on-premise stack versus ABAP in the cloud depends on several factors, including your organization's infrastructure, strategic goals, and specific project requirements. Here are some considerations to help you decide:

 

  1. Tight Integration & Heavy Processing: Industries with very specialized processes often rely on on-premise ABAP applications for highly customized solutions that integrate tightly with ERP and other legacy systems and equipment.

 

  1. Data Sensitivity: If your industry has strict data residency or compliance requirements, keeping sensitive data within an on-premise system may be preferable.

 

  1. Legacy Systems: Organizations with legacy SAP ECC systems are more likely to continue using on-premise ABAP until they complete a migration to SAP S/4HANA.

 

  1. Limited Internet Access: Situations where reliable internet connectivity cannot be guaranteed may favour local, on-premise solutions.

 

Ultimately, the choice depends on your organization's strategic direction, technical landscape, and specific project needs. In most cases, Outside of the above scenarios ABAP on-cloud is the choice.

Step 6 – Multiple BTP Services for Complex Requirements

If requirements don't fit in any of the above criteria, then it might require a mix of SAP BTP services as the requirement may be complex.

 

Conclusion

Based on question responses, each use-case above is evaluated to select appropriate SAP BTP services. This method offers structured guidance on likely required services for a specific use-case.

 

Primarily designed for functional teams, it also supports technical teams new to SAP BTP Services by introducing architectural patterns. Previously, custom applications mainly relied on options like ABAP and ‘Z’ tables. However, the cloud environment provides numerous services with faster updates than on-premise solutions, making basic knowledge of SAP BTP Services essential for consultants.

 

This article aims to facilitate quick identification of suitable SAP BTP services for a given requirement through qualifying questions. Feedback is encouraged in the comments. Thank you, and look forward to the next blog in the series discussing how to select SAP BTP services in Data Modernization Archetype. 

Your feedback welcome, please write to cbcoe@sap.com.

entry.title: What’s New Preview Available for SAP Analytics Cloud Q1 2025
entry.content:
In Q3 2022, we introduced the What’s New Viewer, which made it easier for you to search for and view a list of new and updated features. Published with each SAP Analytics Cloud quarterly release in February, May, August, and November, the What's New Viewer helps you stay informed on the latest changes. 

Since the What’s New View was introduced, one key piece of feedback that we received is that customers want a way to anticipate and prepare for potential changes in advance of each new release.  

We are excited to announce that starting with the Q1 2025 release, the What's New Viewer will include a preview of upcoming changes, including new features, updates, and deprecations. 

What will this look like?  

As of the Q1 2025 release, you will be able to easily see the upcoming features thanks to a Preview flag.  

To refine the content that is displayed, you can use the filters. For example, by applying the Version filter, only the preview content for the upcoming release will be displayed. Also, if you’re interested in a specific category, you can select a Category filter to display preview content for that category.  

When will the preview be available?  

The preview of the What’s New Viewer will be available up to one month before each upcoming quarterly release. For the Q1 2025 release, the preview for What’s New Viewer is available now.

entry.title: Re: SAP Certification Transformation: How You Can Get and Stay Certified
entry.content:
Dear Suzanne,

Before taking the stay-certified assessment, the expiration date was already 31.03.2025. We need to expire more than 31,03.2025. To do this, what will do? Besides that, I faced another problem. As part of the Stay Certified program, our team members have completed the required self-paced courses, which are prerequisites for taking the assessment to renew their SAP certifications and update their expiration dates. However, despite completing these courses, the system still marks them as incomplete. I attached a screenshot of them. Looking forward to your assistance.

entry.title: Re: Calculation Scenario Filter does not work as expected
entry.content:
I don't understand on which assumptions you expectation is based on. Perhaps you can elaborate a bit. Looking at your insert statements column Col_202501 is filled with empty strings.

If you run a SELECT statement directly on the table like:

select * from "src" where not "Col_202501" in ('1') or "Col_202501" is Null

the not=1 returns records

If you run

select * from "src" where "Col_202501" in ('1') or "Col_202501" is Null

Col_202501 is neither 1 nor null so no records are returned.

This column is mapped to VALUE in your calculation view and therefore I would not expect 

in (\"VALUE\", ''1'') or isNull(\"VALUE\")"

to return records.

entry.title: Re: SAP XI/PI excel to custom RFC
entry.content:
That's also my idea: XI/PO caches the function module signature (importing/exporting parameters). If the FM has been changed, but XI/PO not yet updated, then the new parameters are not yet known on XI/PO side, and the RFC protocol in such a case is designed to just ignore unknown data.

I know that for example in the SAP Business Connector (which is similar to XI/PO) there is a button "Clear DDIC Cache", which can be used precisely in those cases. The FM signature is deleted from the cache and then looked up again from the backend DDIC, when it's needed the next time.

I would assume that XI/PO has a similar "Clear Cache" feature. If not, you will have to restart the XI/PO system...

entry.title: Optimizing the Code GROUP BY in loop
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

Can i know an alternate way of using the below code which was existing already in the system.

We observed that this statement causes the performance issues.

Kindly guide me the same. Thanks.

      LOOP AT lt_sa_from_init_screen ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<sa>)
        GROUP BY (  vkorg = <sa>-vkorg
                    vtweg = <sa>-vtweg )
        WITHOUT MEMBERS ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<sa_group>).
        INSERT VALUE mdg_ecc_bp_vkorg_vtweg_key(
          vkorg = <sa_group>-vkorg
          vtweg = <sa_group>-vtweg ) INTO TABLE lt_sales_keys.
      ENDLOOP.

 

Regards,

Karthik s

entry.title: Request in SAP
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

 

How can we set or what can we do so that in SAP the requirements are no longer fixed in the past?
All requirements should be dated (necessary) in the future, not in the past.

 

Thank you all
